title: Secants, Tangents, and Angle Measures

summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

Two chords intersect inside a circle. The intercepted arcs measure 80° and 40°. What is the angle?

Key circle theorems: a central angle equals its intercepted arc; an inscribed angle = ½ its intercepted arc; two chords intersecting inside form angles = ½(sum of arcs). The answer is 60°. The other options — '120°', '20°', and '40°' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

Two tangents from an external point intercept arcs of 250° and 110°. What is the angle between the tangents?

When two chords intersect inside a circle, the angle = ½(sum of intercepted arcs). Also, the products of their segments are equal: (segment₁)(segment₂) = (segment₃)(segment₄). The answer is '70°'. The other options — '180°', '140°', and '55°' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
